parts: Belvala lucens, also known as the slippery elm, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The medicinal parts of Belvala lucens are primarily the inner bark and the roots, which contain mucilages that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The inner bark, in particular, is often used to treat digestive issues and irritable bowel syndrome due to its ability to protect and soothe the mucous membranes of the digestive tract. The roots, on the other hand, have been traditionally used to treat coughs and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Belvala lucens make it a popular herbal remedy for a variety of health issues.
